key_insight: Adding Fréchet Distance (FD) loss to diffusion-based medical image generators helps them better preserve irregular tumor shapes and variability in synthetic images.
clinical_relevance: FD-regularized synthetic images improved downstream tumor segmentation performance, suggesting a practical way to make generated medical data more useful for clinical imaging workflows.
method: Finetuned diffusion generative models with FD-loss instead of relying only on per-pixel denoising loss.
Matched first- and second-order feature statistics between real and generated images in a pretrained encoder space.
Tested the approach across liver and brain cancer datasets in both CT and MRI, using natural-image and medical-image encoders.
evidence_signal: Across multiple datasets and model settings, segmentation networks trained on FD-regularized synthetic data achieved more than 5% higher tumor DSC than unregularized synthetic augmentation.
watch_item: Results depend on the choice of pretrained encoder and dataset; gains in image realism and segmentation may not transfer equally across all tumor types or imaging protocols.
paper_title: Improving Medical Image Generative Models with Fréchet Distance Loss
